data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-72cf3b8d">
    <title>docs(TD-031): codify push_failed/push_conflict outcome rows + cooldown invariant</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  TD-031 §5 outcome→Extra mapping table is missing rows for
  push_failed and push_conflict. Today's implementation parks
  push_failed at 24h cooldown (cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:272-283),
  which violates the §5 invariant: "execute-loop-retry-after may be
  set only when retrying the same bead after time passes could
  plausibly succeed without human/spec/dependency changes."

  push_failed is caused by branch protection, auth-token expiry,
  pre-push test failures (which require code fixes), or a data race
  in executor.go (also a code fix). NONE of these are recheckable
  by time elapsed alone. Without the spec row, implementers add
  cooldowns that survive long after their cause is resolved. Today's
  drain idled ~10h because of stale push_failed cooldowns set during
  a data-race incident that was fixed within the hour.

  This is a doc-only bead. It lands the spec row + the invariant
  before the implementation fixes (sister beads handle code).

CURRENT STATE
  - docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
    §5 has rows for review_pass, already_satisfied, review_fixable_gap,
    review_spec_gap, review_too_large, review_error, execution_failed,
    no_changes (multiple variants), parent/epic, external blocker,
    superseded, transient infra/quota/transport, stale no_changes
    metadata.
  - No row for push_failed.
  - No row for push_conflict.
  - The §5 preamble invariant about execute-loop-retry-after is
    correctly stated but lacks a concrete enumeration of which
    outcomes may set the field vs which must not.

PROPOSED FIX
  Edit docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md
  §5:

  1. Add row for push_failed:
     - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released).
     - Label changes: optional explanatory label (e.g.,
       blocked-on-branch-protection if applicable).
     - Events: push-failed with stderr detail and base-rev.
     - Extra updates: Extra["last-run"] only; DO NOT set
       execute-loop-retry-after. Operator action OR a code fix on
       origin clears the implicit block.

  2. Add row for push_conflict (remote diverged, fast-forward
     rejected by non-FF):
     - Status transition: in_progress → open (claim released).
     - Label changes: (none).
     - Events: push-conflict.
     - Extra updates: may set execute-loop-retry-after at 15min
       (recheckable — remote advanced; a brief wait + re-fetch
       resolves naturally).

  3. Strengthen the §5 invariant prose:
     - Add an explicit list of outcomes that MUST NOT set
       execute-loop-retry-after: push_failed,
       declined_needs_decomposition, review_spec_gap,
       review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap.
     - Note: push_conflict, no_viable_provider, land_conflict,
       transient infra/quota/transport ARE recheckable and may
       use cooldowns.
     - Reinforce: cooldown lifetime should match the recheckable
       window (15min for transient, NOT 24h).

NON-SCOPE
  - Implementation fixes (sister beads).
  - Bulk operator clear command (sister bead).
  - Rev-bound auto-invalidation logic (sister bead).
  - Other sections of TD-031.

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "fix(try): push_failed must not set cooldown" (R1)
    depends on this row landing first.
  - Sister bead "fix(try): declined_needs_decomposition use
    execution-eligible=false" (R4) is referenced by the new
    invariant prose.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md §5 contains a new row for push_failed declaring "in_progress → open" transition, no execute-loop-retry-after, and event push-failed.
2. The same §5 contains a new row for push_conflict declaring "in_progress → open", optional 15min execute-loop-retry-after (recheckable cooldown), and event push-conflict.
3. The §5 preamble invariant prose explicitly enumerates outcomes that MUST NOT set execute-loop-retry-after, and outcomes that MAY (with the recheckable-window rationale).
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:docs, area:agent, kind:doc, spec:TD-031, prevention</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="TD-031" base-rev="7a710a8cf90e72ac173bcaf06889ddc069950051" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260510T160603-2192ced2"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="TD-031" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md">Technical Design: Bead State Machine and Naming-Role Decisions</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.message: ddx-72cf3b8d # 1     +4s tool `{command="git add docs/helix/02-design/...", description="Stage doc and run pre-commit...
data.command: {command="git add docs/helix/02-design/...", description="Stage doc and run pre-commit ..."}

data.message: ddx-72cf3b8d # 1     +3s tool `{command="git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n...", description="Commit TD-031 doc updates"...

data.output:
All four ACs are satisfied:

1. **§5 `push_failed` row** — `in_progress → open`, `Extra["last-run"]` only, explicit "DO NOT set `execute-loop-retry-after`", event `push-failed` with stderr detail and base-rev.
2. **§5 `push_conflict` row** — `in_progress → open`, optional 15-min `execute-loop-retry-after`, event `push-conflict`.
3. **§5 invariant prose** — now enumerates outcomes that MUST NOT set the retry-after field (`push_failed`, `declined_needs_decomposition`, `review_spec_gap`, `review_missing_acceptance`, `review_too_large` at depth cap) and outcomes that MAY set it with the recheckable-window rationale and 15-min ceiling; 24 h cooldown explicitly ruled out.
4. **`lefthook run pre-commit` passes** — confirmed above.
